code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Negative updates that erase previous satisfaction (even if initial purchase was good)
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional consequences

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Comparative inferiority without safety hazards

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function (washing, seasoning, etc.)
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Usage limitations affecting functionality
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Material flaws impacting durability but not safety
    - Functional flaws resolved after initial use

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise with concrete benefits
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (durability/design/utility)
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close comparisons to top brands with daily-driver status
    - Practical benefits significantly outweigh limitations
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" endorsements despite imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Praise + functional flaw →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if used regularly despite differences
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless execution
    - Negative updates override previous positive experiences →1
    - Brief functional praise →4 unless accompanied by enthusiastic validation
    - Material maintenance needs →5 if non-functional impact
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: First purchase lasted 3 years, replacement failed in 2 months!"
<Reasoning>Negative update with multi-purchase failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise with specific benefit →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with daily use →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Had plastic smell initially, but works great after 2 washes"
<Reasoning>Preprocessing required for full function →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Assembly took muscle but resulted in rock-solid bed frame"
<Reasoning>Assembly difficulty despite final satisfaction →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for light organizing. Thin material worries me"
<Reasoning>Adequate with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Between kids/pets, this cleaner surprised me daily!"
<Reasoning>Exceeds expectations despite initial doubts →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Rollers jam weekly but handle bumps when working"
<Reasoning>Persistent maintenance needs →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Non-stick sprays off after 3 washes. Still my favorite pan"
<Reasoning>Functional flaw outweighed by core benefit →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Works but needs constant adjustment. Exhausting"
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (35 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 5
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 5 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 1, 3] | Disagreement: 0.67
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 35 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
